title: POST /api/auth/logout — clear the session cookie
problem: The sign-out side of the auth flow. mp_session and mp_csrf cookies are cleared via Set-Cookie max-age=0; the response carries both clears + a JSON ok. Stateless because the cookie IS the session — there's no server-side session table to revoke. CSRF-exempt because a forced logout is a nuisance, not an account compromise, and gating it would create chicken-and-egg problems if the SPA's mp_csrf has drifted.
analysis: Single POST handler that writes Set-Cookie headers clearing mp_session and mp_csrf (max-age=0, same path/domain attributes as the original). Returns 200 { ok: true }. No D1 reads, no D1 writes — there's nothing to revoke server-side. The session is the cookie; clearing the cookie ends the session. CSRF is intentionally exempt — see the in-handler comment for why (forced logout is benign, gating it creates failure modes when CSRF cookie has drifted). Sentry doesn't wrap this handler specifically; the middleware does for any uncaught exceptions.

decisions.rationale: Server-side session tables exist for systems where you need 'log out everywhere' or 'revoke this device' as features. This portal's sessions are short-lived (7 days), low-stakes, and the user can already invalidate them by clearing browser cookies. Adding a session table for the sake of API symmetry would add a table to maintain, a revocation list to query on every authenticated request, and zero real-world security benefit at this scale.
decisions.recommendation: Don't add a session table. If 'log out from all devices' ever becomes a real ask, the right answer is to rotate SESSION_SECRET (which invalidates all existing signed cookies in one operation, server-side, instantly).

decisions.rationale: The CSRF gate exists to prevent a logged-in user from being tricked into making a state-changing request. Forced logout via cross-site POST is a nuisance (the user has to re-sign-in) but doesn't compromise their account. Gating /api/auth/logout would create a real failure mode: a user whose mp_csrf has drifted (cookie expired but mp_session valid) cannot sign out — they're stuck. The exemption is documented in _middleware.ts CSRF_EXEMPT_PATHS.
decisions.recommendation: Keep the exemption. If a future review wants to gate it, the better fix is to surface 'your CSRF is stale, get a new one' as an explicit re-mint endpoint — not to require it for logout.

successCriteria:
- A POST clears mp_session and mp_csrf via Set-Cookie max-age=0
- Response is 200 { ok: true } with no body data leaks
- No D1 reads or writes
- CSRF-exempt status is preserved in CSRF_EXEMPT_PATHS
- After this call, the next authenticated request to /api/me returns { email: null }
implementation: functions/api/auth/logout.ts — onRequestPost. Two Set-Cookie headers via Headers.append (clearSessionCookieHeader + clearCsrfCookieHeader from functions/_lib/auth.ts), single JSON response. ~10 lines. Tests: covered by the auth integration tests in functions/api/me.test.ts (logout-then-GET flow).
userGuide: Server-side endpoint. The SPA calls this via /me's 'sign out' link. Returns 200 { ok: true }; the cookies are cleared by the browser per the Set-Cookie headers. To trigger manually: curl -X POST http://localhost:8788/api/auth/logout -b 'mp_session=...; mp_csrf=...'. To force-sign-out a specific user without a request from them: rotate SESSION_SECRET via `wrangler secret put SESSION_SECRET` — invalidates every existing session in one operation.
knownLimitations: No 'log out from all devices' — clearing the cookie only ends THIS browser's session; another device with a valid mp_session keeps working. Acceptable for this scale; SESSION_SECRET rotation is the nuclear option
Stateless means we have no record of 'when did this user last sign out' — useful for audit logs but absent today

If a session has been hijacked, clearing the cookie on the victim's browser doesn't invalidate the attacker's copy of the cookie — the attacker keeps their session until expiry. Mitigation: rotate SESSION_SECRET

statusHistory.reason: Endpoint shipped with both cookie clears + 200 response.
Code-first doc co-located. Stateless model and CSRF exemption are stable.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Initial author — P2 critical-API sweep, co-located doc for /api/auth/logout.
